_README: User/counsel-maintained statute-of-limitations references. This is the OVERRIDE layer for recupero.legal.limitations: entries here win over the small seeded baseline (US federal criminal periods) and are the place a firm records the CONTROLLING civil/criminal limitation periods for the jurisdictions it works. Keys are jurisdiction names (free-form; normalized — 'United States'/'USA'/'US' all map to US; 'United Kingdom'/'UK' -> UK; also EU, CA, AU, SG). Each value is a LIST of references. SAFETY: every entry MUST carry a real 'citation' and a valid 'claim_kind' (criminal|civil) and a 'period' — the loader DROPS (and warns on) any entry missing them, so a period is never shipped without its statute. Set verified=true ONLY with a 'source'; the loader downgrades a sourceless verified entry. 'verified' means the CITATION is confirmed against the statute, NOT that the period governs a given case (accrual/tolling/which offense is always counsel's call — the rendered advisory says so). This is legal INFORMATION, not legal advice.

<Jurisdiction>.label: string — human display, e.g. 'Federal wire fraud (general)'
<Jurisdiction>.period: string — e.g. '5 years' or '6 years, or 2 years from discovery'
<Jurisdiction>.citation: string — REQUIRED real statutory citation (entry dropped without it)
<Jurisdiction>.accrual: string|null — when the clock starts / discovery-rule note
<Jurisdiction>.illustrative: boolean — true if this is a jurisdiction EXAMPLE, not the controlling period
<Jurisdiction>.verified: boolean — true ONLY when the citation is confirmed and a source is recorded

United Kingdom.label: Tort/contract (example placeholder — confirm)
United Kingdom.accrual: From the date the cause of action accrued (fraud/concealment may postpone — s.32).
United Kingdom.note: EXAMPLE ONLY — confirm the controlling period and any s.32 postponement with counsel.
United Kingdom.source: confirm against the Limitation Act 1980 before relying on it
